title: Reduction to R_n^{(1,1)} via Bergeron + diagonal supersymmetry

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Sub-lemma G1.** A complete solution must rigorously justify the reduction of the hook-coefficient problem for the multigraded coinvariant ring $R_n^{(m)}$ to a bigraded computation in the superspace coinvariant ring $R_n^{(1,1)}$.

Specifically, prove (by correctly invoking the cited theorems and checking their hypotheses) that:

1. **(Bergeron \cite{Bergeron2020})** $\mathrm{Hilb}(R_n^{(m)};q_1,\dots,q_m)=\sum_\lambda c_\lambda(n)s_\lambda(q_1,\dots,q_m)$ with $c_\lambda(n)\ge0$ **independent of $m$**, and *all* coefficients $c_\lambda(n)$ that can ever appear already appear once $m\ge n$. Hence one may reduce to $m=n$.

2. **(Diagonal supersymmetry, Lentfer \cite{Lentfer-Supersymmetry}, Prop. `\ref{prop:ds}`)** The same universal coefficients satisfy
$$\mathrm{Hilb}(R_n^{(1,1)};q;u)=\sum_{\lambda\in P(1,1,n)} c_\lambda(n)\,s_\lambda(q/u),$$
and $P(1,1,n)$ (partitions with $\lambda_2\le1$, $\ell(\lambda)\le n$) is **exactly** the set of hook partitions of length $\le n$.

The hypothesis to verify is that the hook coefficients are precisely those captured by the super-coinvariant expansion, so that solving for $c_{(a,1^b)}(n)$ in $R_n^{(1,1)}$ answers the original question for all $m$.

This is an *invocation* gap (deep external theorems are cited, not reproved), but the hypotheses and the identification $P(1,1,n)=\{$hooks$\}$ must be stated and checked explicitly rather than asserted in passing.
